channel.description: Latest marine jobs for Yachts Jobs
channel.title: Maritime Union - Job for Mariners

item.description:
Location: Netherlands
Description:

📍 Location: North Netherlands
📅 Start Date: ASAP
📄 Contract: Dutch temporary agency contract
⏳ Hours: 40 hrs/week guaranteed + possible overtime

💡 Your Tasks:

✅ Install, fit & secure piping systems for fresh water, seawater, fuel & hydraulics on yachts
✅ Measure, cut, thread & bend pipes using specialized tools (pipe benders, cutters, welding torches)
✅ Perform leak tests, hydrostatic tests & inspections to ensure system integrity
✅ Conduct maintenance, troubleshooting & repairs on piping systems (valves, pumps, fittings)
✅ Read & interpret blueprints, isometric drawings & piping schematics
✅ Work with marine-grade materials (stainless steel, CuNi, PEX piping)

🔥 What We Offer:

🏠 Free housing (single room in shared apartment)
🚗 Company car provided (or km allowance if using own car)
✈️ First & last flight paid (rotation travel at worker’s expense)
💰 Weekly salary payments
🩺 Health insurance arranged by Wanted and deducted from your growth salary
🔄 Preferred rotation: 6 weeks work / 2 weeks home (flexible)

🦺 Bring your own safety shoes (S3 over ankle), other PPE provided by Wanted, if needed

🔎 Requirements:

✔️ Conversational English required
✔️ VCA & BSN (We can help you obtain them if needed)
✔️ Driving license (Advantage)

📩 Interested? Apply now!

Countries: Netherlands

Languages: English



Apply to this job
item.title: [Hot Job] Pipefitters for Yachts at WANTED Spijkenisse

item.title: [AI Job] AB Coxswain on SOV - STCW Required at Select Offshore

item.description:
Location:
Description:

Bridgemans Services Group ( Bridgemans Crew Management Ltd.) is a global provider of workforce floatels and marine services. BSG customizes services to the exact needs and locations of clients, ensuring solutions meet precise specifications, including logistics, crew transfers, housekeeping, catering, and the fulfillment of all HSE requirements. 

The Opportunity

The Deckhand of the passenger transfers vessel. Main duties related to the safe transfer of personnel, and emergency response as required. Ensure the safety of life, the vessel, and the prevention of marine pollution while working within the company's established health and safety guidelines, policies, and procedures.

Key Responsibilities

Responsibilities and essential job functions include, but are not limited to, the following:

Engine Operation:

  • Responsible for operating and controlling the ship engine, propulsion systems, and auxiliary equipment to ensure the vessel's proper and efficient functioning during voyages.

Maintenance and Repair

  • Perform routine maintenance, inspections, and repairs on mechanical, electrical, and hydraulic systems to prevent breakdowns and malfunctions. Including engines, generators, pumps, and other essential machinery.

Safety and Compliance

  • Responsible for conducting safety drills and maintaining safety equipment.

Watchkeeping: 

  • Continuous watch over the vessel's machinery and systems, monitoring for any irregularities or issues that may arise.

Troubleshooting

  • Responsible for diagnosing issues and implementing appropriate solutions promptly and efficiently.

Record-Keeping

  • Maintain detailed records of maintenance, repairs, fuel consumption, and other important data related to the vessel's operation.

Environmental Compliance

  • Oversee the proper disposal of waste, fuel usage, and compliance with environmental regulations to minimize the ship's impact on the marine ecosystem.

Emergency Response

  • In case of emergencies, ship engineers play a vital role in responding to situations such as fires, flooding, or other crises, working with the rest of the crew to manage and resolve the issue.

Training and Guidance

  • Senior ship engineers often provide guidance and training to junior engineering staff to ensure the proper transfer of knowledge and skills.

Communication

  • Effective communication with the ship's officers, crew members, and other engineering staff is crucial to maintain smooth operations and address any engineering-related concerns or tasks.

Qualifications

  • Possessing an SVMO-U qualification
  • Must have sailed as Deckhand on motor ships.
  • Valid Seafarers Medical certificate
  • Valid STCW courses
  • Advanced First Aid an asset
  • Ability to clear a drug and alcohol test prior to hiring and potential random tests throughout employment with prior notice.
  • Reliable mode of transportation & valid class 5 driver’s license
  • Fluent communication in English

Working Conditions

  • Frequent standing, bending, twisting, and walking throughout the shift. 
  • Frequent situations where maintaining balance is essential.
  • Frequent kneeling
  • Exposure to environmental elements including extreme weather conditions.
  • Frequent lifting, pulling pushing up to 50lbs.
  • Occasional ascending or descending ladders, stairs, and ramps 

At Bridgemans Services Group we believe that each employee contributes directly to our growth and success. We are committed to workplace excellence, safe work environments and the communities where we work and live. Bridgemans Services Group hires on the basis of merit and is committed to Employment equity and development.

Primary Location: Howe Sound, BC
Employee Status: Full-time/Rotational Contract
Rate Range (Daily): $378.00 to $420.00


Apply to this job
item.title: [Hot Job] Deckhand with SVMO $378.00 to $420.00 (Daily) at Bridgemans Crew Management

item.description:
Location:
Description:

Bridgemans Services Group ( Bridgemans Crew Management Ltd.) is a global provider of workforce floatels and marine services. BSG customizes services to the exact needs and locations of clients, ensuring solutions meet precise specifications, including logistics, crew transfers, housekeeping, catering, and the fulfillment of all HSE requirements. 


The Opportunity

Deckhand of the passenger transfers vessel. Main duties related to the safe transfer of personnel, and emergency response as required. Ensure the safety of life, the vessel, and the prevention of marine pollution while working within the established health and safety guidelines, policies, and procedures of the Company.

Key Responsibilities

Responsibilities and essential job functions include, but are not limited to, the following:

  • Lookout duties as assigned by vessel Master/Operator.
  • Safety and security rounds as assigned.
  • Safety of passengers during embarking, disembarking, and when underway
  • Verifying that all crew and passengers have all required Personal Protective Equipment (PPE).
  • Assisting in loading/unloading equipment/cargo to and from the vessel both onshore and offshore.
  • Perform passenger safety briefings. 
  • Monitor transfer of client cargo and record as required.
  • Complete passenger counts and report to Master.
  • Operate crane and perform cargo handling (Rigger/Banksman) if certified, as required.
  • Deck duties as directed by Master/vessel operator, completed safety checks, engine checks, vessel husbandry, vessel interior, and exterior cleaning, and sanitation.

Qualifications

  • Relevant marine experience preferred.
  • MED-DVS (Marine Emergency Duties training program for Canadian seafarers working on domestic vessels)
  • Radio Operators Certificate – Marine (ROC-M)
  • Marine Basic or Advanced First Aid (MBFA) / (MAFA)
  • Valid Seafarers Medical certificate (MMC)
  • Excellent comprehension and command of the English language (written & verbal)
  • Ability to clear a drug and alcohol test prior to hiring, in addition, to randomly with prior notice.
  • Bridge Watch rating an asset.

Working Conditions

  • Frequent standing, bending, twisting, and walking throughout the shift. 
  • Frequent situations where maintaining balance is essential
  • Frequent kneeling
  • Exposure to environmental elements including extreme weather conditions.
  • Frequent lifting, pulling pushing up to 50lbs
  • Occasional ascending or descending ladders, stairs, and ramps 

At Bridgemans Services Group we believe that each employee contributes directly to our growth and success. We are committed to workplace excellence, safe work environments, and the communities where we work and live. Bridgemans Services Group hires on the basis of merit and is committed to Employment equity and development.

Primary Location: Howe Sound
Employee Status: Full-time/Rotational Contract
Salary Range (Day): $378.00 to $420.00


Apply to this job
item.title: [Hot Job] Deckhand $378.00 to $420.00 (Day) at Bridgemans Crew Management

item.description:
Location: Worldwide
Description:

We have the pleasure to introduce ourselves as a Crewing company in Klaipeda, Lithuania. Company Gretimybe was founded in 1997 by a group of specialists experienced in crewing, shipping and sea business. Today Gretimybe is the biggest crewing company in Baltic States. 

We are looking for:

  • Position: Stewardess
  • Salary: 2800 USD p/m
  • Vessel Type: Pleasure Craft
  • Contract duration: 2 month(s)
  • Requirement(s): Experience in rank; Good English.

If you are interested with this job – click the "Apply here" button and send your CV.



Apply to this job
item.title: [Hot Job] Stewardess for Pleasure Boat with salary 2800 USD at Gretimybe
